Front: AWS: what is the flagship managed ML service, and what is the ML lifecycle it supports?
Back: Amazon SageMaker - a suite covering the whole ML lifecycle (Data Wrangler/Processing, Studio + Training, Endpoints for serving, Model Registry, Pipelines, Model Monitor). AWS ML lifecycle: Business Goal Identification -> ML Problem Framing -> Data Processing -> Model Development -> Deployment -> Monitoring -> loop back. Same iterative shape as MLflow's track/register/serve and the eval scientific-method loop.

Front: Why does CI default to loading the model from a local file instead of the MLflow registry? (hermetic CI)
Back: Because the CI runner cannot reach an MLflow registry (no server, and it can't reach your laptop). CI must be HERMETIC - self-contained and reproducible, depending only on the repo/generated artifacts. So it runs train_model.py to make a local model.joblib and loads that. Registry mode is only for real serving, toggled via MODEL_URI. (Not to be confused with the image-tag version bug.)

Front: MLflow: minimum steps to promote a new model version to production?
Back: Register the new model as a version (v2) in the registry -> move the alias (@champion) to v2 (UI or set_registered_model_alias) -> restart the app (it loads the model at startup). No app code change or redeploy. Register -> realias -> restart.

Front: Fixed-size vs variable-size sliding window: the signal?
Back: Fixed: the target length is given/known, so the window stays that width (max-sum subarray of size k; a permutation of s1 = fixed length). Variable: you're optimizing a length under a condition, so the window grows/shrinks (longest substring without repeating characters).
